objective: Reduce chant to a thin runtime shell that only handles routing, session lifecycle, and scheduler handoff while delegating planning, compression, and worker context construction to dedicated weaves.
problem_statement: The current chant weave mixes solid direct-command routing with an unfinished sequential planning loop. Runtime behavior, tests, and planning artifacts have drifted: direct routes work, but planning envelopes no longer match the tests, the Architect is not yet the global filter, and the swarm-native thread model has not been materialized.
principles: Chant is the entrypoint, not the planner.
The Architect is the only component that sees the global PennyOne Matrix.
The Hall stores static truth; episodic memory stores tactical truth.
Workers receive only focused RAM, never the global project schema.
Session envelopes, tests, and runtime behavior must describe the same contract.

beads.title: Reality Reset: Stabilize the Chant Contract
beads.description: Define the authoritative responsibility boundary for chant. Keep direct routing for built-in weaves and installed skills, but replace the ambiguous planning contract with an explicit shell contract. Either restore `NEEDS_INPUT` and `PLAN_READY` deliberately or update the tests and metadata to the new `PROPOSAL_REVIEW` model, but do not leave a split-brain system in place.
acceptance_criteria: Direct command routing remains green.
Planning responses expose a stable metadata contract.
No test expects a legacy state that the runtime no longer emits.
No placeholder planning mode is described as production-ready.

beads.title: Phase 2 Reopen: Context Compressor and Episodic Memory
beads.description: Implement `weave:compress` using the ONE MIND delegation pattern with strict `Model Hint: gemini-2.5-flash-lite`. Add `hall_episodic_memory` persistence and the TypeScript contracts needed to store tactical summaries of completed bead work. The compressor should accept bead intent plus a diff or equivalent artifact envelope and emit strict JSON that records only successful tactical outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: `weave:compress` exists and is registered.
The weave emits a `[SUB_AGENT_DIRECTIVE]` with `Model Hint: gemini-2.5-flash-lite`.
`hall_episodic_memory` exists in the runtime schema and has save/read helpers.
There are focused unit tests for compressor delegation and episodic memory persistence.

beads.title: Architect-Centric Proposal Builder
beads.description: Move mission synthesis out of chant. The Architect must consume the user intent, research artifacts, and Hall schema, then emit a dependency-aware bead graph plus a proposal summary. Chant should request this graph, not build it internally.
acceptance_criteria: Architect owns proposal synthesis.
Architect emits a structured dependency graph, not a linear placeholder plan.
Chant stops embedding proposal-construction logic beyond dispatch and state transitions.

beads.title: Chant as Main-Thread Scheduler
beads.description: Shrink chant into a session shell and scheduler. It should create or resume the planning session, dispatch the Architect to obtain the bead graph, and then manage handoff to the execution layer. Remove the monolithic sequential planning loop and keep only the shell responsibilities that belong in the main thread.
acceptance_criteria: Chant is no longer the place where proposal logic, critique reasoning, and worker context are computed.
Session bootstrap/resume still works cleanly from CLI and TUI.
Chant becomes a shell around scheduling and routing, not a catch-all planner.

beads.title: Focused Sub-Thread RAM Builder
beads.description: Construct a focused RAM packet for worker threads. The packet must contain only tactical instructions, compressed episodic memories of direct dependencies, and relevant Hall file references. Do not pass the global PennyOne Matrix or broad project schema into worker execution.
acceptance_criteria: Worker payloads include only dependency memories and relevant file slices.
Global schema stays with the Architect.
There is a deterministic packet shape for sub-thread startup.

beads.title: Parallel Dependency Scheduler and Recovery Rules
beads.description: Teach the shell scheduler to dispatch all graph nodes whose dependencies are satisfied. Failures must not poison unrelated threads. Recovery must be bounded and explicit: retry within policy, compress only successful work, and invalidate downstream dependents when an upstream bead fails materially.
acceptance_criteria: Independent beads can run in parallel.
Dependency barriers are enforced.
Failure handling is bounded and downstream invalidation is explicit.
Episodic memory records only successful tactical progress.

beads.title: Observability and Contract Hardening
beads.description: Lock the new architecture down with tests and operator-visible state. Add focused coverage for chant shell behavior, architect graph output, compressor delegation, episodic memory writes, parallel scheduling, and session resume semantics. Remove any remaining ambiguity between documentation, tests, and runtime behavior.
acceptance_criteria: The shell contract is covered by tests.
Parallel execution behavior is covered by tests.
Operator-facing docs and skill metadata match the actual architecture.
No stale planning artifact claims completion for work that is still pending.
